title: Tesla's Deposition in the Marconi Patent Case (1916)
description: Court testimony where Tesla explains his reasoning behind specific technical decisions in wireless technology. Under legal pressure to be precise, reveals his actual thought process behind inventions.

title: Tesla's World's Fair Lectures (1893) - Complete Transcripts
description: Transcripts of Tesla's demonstrations and explanations at the Chicago World's Fair, showing how he reasoned about public presentation of complex ideas and his criteria for what to demonstrate.

title: Tesla's Wardenclyffe Correspondence with J.P. Morgan (1901-1906)
description: Letters documenting Tesla's struggle between commercial viability and scientific vision. Shows how he reasoned about compromising his wireless transmission goals for financial backing, revealing his prioritization process.

title: Tesla's AC Motor Development Crisis (1887-1888) - Technical Papers and Letters
description: Documentation of Tesla's struggle to solve rotating magnetic field problems, showing his iterative reasoning, dead-end approaches, and breakthrough moments. Reveals how he processed technical failures.

title: Tesla's Oscillator Experiments - Failure Analysis from Technical Journals
description: Contemporary technical reports documenting Tesla's mechanical oscillator experiments that caused building vibrations, showing his response to unexpected consequences and risk assessment processes.

title: Tesla's Personal Letters to Robert Underwood Johnson (1899-1922)
description: Correspondence with his close friend and editor, revealing Tesla's private thoughts on business decisions, scientific priorities, and personal philosophy. Shows his reasoning about patent strategies and research directions.

title: Tesla's Laboratory Notes and Journals (Colorado Springs, 1899-1900)
description: Day-by-day experimental records showing Tesla's hypothesis formation, testing protocols, and iterative reasoning. Reveals how he processed failures and adjusted experimental approaches.

title: The Electrical World Interview Series (1894-1899)
description: Series of technical interviews where Tesla explains his reasoning behind various inventions. Less guarded than formal publications, revealing his actual thought processes and decision criteria.

title: Tesla's Later Years: The Poverty and Isolation Period Letters (1920-1943)
description: Personal correspondence during his final decades revealing how his decision-making evolved under financial and social pressure. Shows adaptation of his reasoning frameworks to changed circumstances.

title: My Inventions: The Autobiography of Nikola Tesla
description: Tesla's own account of his life and work, revealing his systematic approach to invention, his visualization methods, and his decision-making process. Contains detailed descriptions of his mental laboratory technique and how he reasoned through technical problems.

title: The Problem of Increasing Human Energy (Century Magazine, 1900)
description: Tesla's comprehensive essay on his philosophical framework for technological progress, revealing his systematic thinking about energy, efficiency, and human advancement. Shows his decision-making criteria for choosing research directions.

title: The Inventions, Researches and Writings of Nikola Tesla
description: Collection of Tesla's technical papers showing his systematic approach to problem-solving and his reasoning about electromagnetic phenomena. Reveals his conceptual frameworks and decision logic.

title: Nikola Tesla: A Biography by John J. O'Neill
description: Written by a science journalist who knew Tesla personally and interviewed him extensively. Contains direct quotes about Tesla's mental methods and decision-making processes, including his visualization techniques.

title: Tesla's Mental Models of Invention by W. Bernard Carlson
description: Academic analysis of Tesla's cognitive processes based on his notebooks and technical papers. Examines his visualization methods and systematic approach to invention as decision-making frameworks.
